current_phase: Phase 2 - Working product shell and focused knowledge layer

current_state.summary: The repo now contains a working Next.js internal training email studio, a file-backed local data layer, provider adapters with mock-safe fallback, seeded Monday and Thursday content, and a focused markdown knowledge system that supports the product shell.

01_google_ai_for_loan_officers.next_priority: Validate current Google AI capabilities against official sources, then expand the lane into a fuller tool map and review-rules set.

02_loan_factory_and_terraplus_operations.next_priority: Load internal workflow artifacts, then replace the current scaffolds with validated process maps and approval rules.

03_sales_marketing_communication_and_con.next_priority: Add reviewed real message examples and upgrade the prompt library once HAPPA is factually defined.

04_weekly_training_production_lab.next_priority: Run one real transcript through the studio and weekly workflow, then tighten the template and topic banks using source grounded evidence.

notes: Dashboard, Draft Studio, and draft detail pages were inspected through a browser-backed Playwright CLI pass.
HTTP checks confirmed live 200 responses for /topics, /drafts/draft-monday-2, and /favicon.ico.
Needs factual validation and Needs compliance review markers were checked across the knowledge layer.

needs_factual_validation: Real company process details and approval owners
Real performance examples and message performance data
Current Google AI product capabilities and internal tool availability
Portal field requirements and automation trigger design
The meaning and intended usage of HAPPA in the Loan Factory context
MOSO and seven-day SLA definitions and approved workflow meaning
needs_compliance_review: Borrower facing, lead facing, and realtor facing templates or messages before live use
Training or communication content that references loan products, rates, approvals, fees, timelines, or borrower scenarios
Operational guides covering rate locks, extensions, rate alerts, or AI-assisted 1003 handling

next_best_move: Take one real Monday topic and one real Thursday topic through the live studio flow with validated source material, then tune the AI prompts, approval notes, and topic banks from Jeremy's feedback.
